hypothesis: Incorporating a Dirichlet-process-based noise schedule into the forward diffusion process—replacing the standard Gaussian noise with noise sampled from a Dirichlet distribution parameterized to respect the simplex geometry of composite compositions—will eliminate the need for post-hoc regularization penalties (ϕnon-neg and ϕsum) while simultaneously improving Density and Coverage metrics beyond those achieved by PCDiff's regularization approach, because the generative process will remain intrinsically confined to the valid compositional simplex at every diffusion timestep rather than being penalized for leaving it.
